hyperedges.label: Podigger CI/CD Pipeline (CI → build images → deploy to staging/production → release)
specs/003-db-optimization: composite indexes + RefreshService + DbAwareTask + bulk-seed fixture + optimization-guard test
Flask → Django migration program (incremental strategy + code mapping + completed v2.1.0 migration)
Podigger Full Stack (Django + Next.js + Postgres + Redis + Celery)
Automated Semantic Versioning Pipeline (Commitizen + Conventional Commits + SemVer + CHANGELOG + GitHub Releases)
Production Deployment Topology (prod compose + base compose + nginx proxy + Cloudflare + Gunicorn)

nodes.label: Dependabot Configuration
nodes.rationale: Single commit-message prefix per ecosystem; dev/infra group ordering required so psycopg2-binary matches before psycopg2.

nodes.label: Backend Dev Dependencies Group (pytest, ruff, commitizen, psycopg2-binary)
nodes.rationale: Must come before backend-infra-dependencies so psycopg2-binary matches before psycopg2.
nodes.norm_label: backend dev dependencies group (pytest, ruff, commitizen, psycopg2-binary)

nodes.label: Backend Infra Dependencies Group (celery, redis, gunicorn, psycopg2)
nodes.norm_label: backend infra dependencies group (celery, redis, gunicorn, psycopg2)

nodes.label: CI test-backend job (pytest, ruff, migrations)
nodes.rationale: Reduces Hypothesis examples in CI (HYPOTHESIS_MAX_EXAMPLES=20) to keep test time reasonable.

nodes.label: CI build-and-push-images job (GHCR Docker publish)
nodes.rationale: Only runs on push to main or develop (not on PRs). Tags with sha-*, branch ref, and 'latest' for main.

nodes.label: Protected Image Tags (latest, main, develop)
nodes.rationale: Never deleted regardless of age to preserve rollback and current release artifacts.

nodes.label: GHCR Image Retention Policy (30 days, dry-run support)
nodes.norm_label: ghcr image retention policy (30 days, dry-run support)

links.evidence: Top horizontal strip separated from content area by visual division
Three small circles (.75 radius) in the top-left area resemble macOS-style window control buttons
SVG shows a rectangular window frame with title bar and content area, fill color #666
A standard application window UI includes a title bar as a sub-component
Application window UIs typically include control buttons in the title bar

hyperedges.label: Podigger CI/CD Pipeline (CI → build images → deploy to staging/production → release)
specs/003-db-optimization: composite indexes + RefreshService + DbAwareTask + bulk-seed fixture + optimization-guard test
Flask → Django migration program (incremental strategy + code mapping + completed v2.1.0 migration)
Podigger Full Stack (Django + Next.js + Postgres + Redis + Celery)
